title: Red Hat Security Advisory: kea security update
overview:
An update for kea is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: kea security update

An update for kea is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for kea is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

CVE-2025-40779: A vulnerability was found in Kea. When an attacker who is an existing client with an assigned IP sends a crafted unicast packet directly to the server's IP and Kea cannot find any subnets that match that client's credentials, the server crashes causing a Denial of Service via assertion/NULL-path failure. This vulnerability does not persist for broadcast packets because they ignore such unverifiable clients.
